description: John Zenon, a construction worker, requires a baseline medical surveillance examination prior to assignment in an area with high respirable crystalline silica dust exposure. As the Occupational Health and Safety Technician, complete his baseline documentation in GNU Health. Document his occupational risk factor using the ICD-10 Z57 family code for occupational exposure to dust. Perform a clinical evaluation documenting his baseline Respiratory Rate and Oxygen Saturation (SpO2). Create a Lifestyle record to document his smoking history and order at least two baseline laboratory tests, such as a CBC and Comprehensive Metabolic Panel. Finally, schedule his mandatory annual surveillance appointment for approximately one year from today (between 350 and 380 days). Login: admin / gnusolidario.
